text: You have three hours (plus any pre-agreed individual adjustment) to complete the paper.
Answer four essay questions, TWO from Section A and TWO from Section B.
Each essay question carries the same number of marks.
Candidates are advised to devote equal time to each question.

This is a closed book exam.
Candidates should not copy and paste images from any other documents.
Where appropriate (e.g. for formulae, equations, or diagrams) you may hand write or draw on the Inspera paper provided.

sub-instructions: ⓘ Answer TWO questions from SECTION A and TWO questions from SECTION B.
content: How do T cells facilitate an effective antibody response?

Describe the role of pattern recognition receptors in the initiation and polarisation of an immune reaction. What are the risks and advantages of pattern recognition receptors being activated by damage-associated molecular patterns in addition to  pathogen-associated molecular patterns?
Chronic virus infections are worse than acute virus infections for the host – discuss.
Using examples, describe how viruses subvert host cells to promote their replication.
Describe the processes that generate antibody diversity. How is self-reactivity avoided during these processes?
Discuss how extracellular bacterial pathogens evade host immune responses.
Describe the mechanisms that govern cell cycle progression and how they are disrupted in cancer cells.
Discuss how intrinsic red blood cell abnormalities may lead to anaemia.
